title: Thalictrum coriaceum
intro: Thalictrum coriaceum, also known as rough meadow-rue, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Ranunculaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and may help to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin issues. The bioactive constituents of Thalictrum coriaceum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Thalictrum coriaceum is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and may be used to make tinctures, infusions, and poultices for topical application.
taxonomy: Thalictrum coriaceum belongs to the family Ranunculaceae, which is commonly known as the buttercup family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its relationship to other plants in the same family. The genus Thalictrum is a group of flowering plants that are often referred to as meadow rue. Within the genus Thalictrum, T. coriaceum is one of several species found in North America. The plant's classification is an important aspect of botany and helps scientists understand its evolution and relationships to other organisms.
